title: Beyond Good and Evil
description: Beyond Good and Evil — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "善恶的彼岸" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: Table of Contents
memes.description: 'Complete table of contents for Nietzsche''s "Beyond Good and Evil"'

memes.label: Affect
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of affect as the primitive reality of mental life — more fundamental than ideas, and the true basis of all moral valuation'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: "Aus hohen Bergen: From High Mountains. Aftersong"
memes.description: "Aftersong: From High Mountains"

memes.label: Appearance
memes.description: "Nietzsche's rehabilitation of appearance (Schein) against the philosophical privileging of 'true reality' — appearance as a condition of life, and the will to appearance as life-affirming force"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Art
memes.description: "Nietzsche's conception of art as a counter-force to nihilism — aesthetic creation as the highest form of human activity and the model for value-creation in all domains"

memes.label: Arthur Schopenhauer
memes.description: 'German philosopher whose pessimism and concept of the will deeply influenced Nietzsche early thought, later subjected to critique in Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Artist
memes.description: "Nietzsche's philosophy of the artist (Künstler) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the artist as creator, transformer, and seducer; the artist's relationship to truth, style, and the philosopher."

memes.label: Awe
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of awe (Ehrfurcht) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the reverence for rank, age, and tradition that distinguishes noble culture from democratic leveling."

memes.label: Bad Conscience
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of bad conscience as the internalization of cruelty and authority that produces guilt, self-punishment, and the moral epoch of human history'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Barbarian
memes.description: "Nietzsche's use of 'barbarian' (Barbar) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the barbarian caste as the origin of nobility, and modern half-barbarism as a creative cultural condition."

memes.label: Baruch Spinoza
memes.description: 'Dutch-Jewish rationalist philosopher whose rejection of free will and teleology Nietzsche admired, discussed in Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Becoming
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s metaphysics of becoming — the rejection of static being, the world as perpetual flux and self-overcoming, and its implications for knowledge and value'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Body
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s philosophy of the body (Leib) as "great reason" — the embodied self against philosophical denigration of the physical, and the body as the ground of perspective and value'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Breeding
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of discipline and breeding (Zucht) as the necessary precondition for the emergence of the noble type — morality itself as an instrument of cultivation'

memes.label: Christianity
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of Christianity as the religion of slave morality — the devaluation of noble values, the poisoning of Eros, and the "most fatal form of self-conceit"'

memes.label: Cleanliness
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of intellectual cleanliness (Reinlichkeit) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the instinct for purity that leads to philosophical solitude and, at its highest, to holiness."

memes.label: Cleverness
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of cleverness (Klugheit) as a form of noble cunning — the art of grace and play that distinguishes the higher type, and the uncanny intelligence that makes humanity a dangerous animal'

memes.label: Command
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of commanding and obeying — the will as an affective structure of command, the social distribution of the commanding disposition, and the moral hypocrisy of modern "commanders"'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Concept
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s meta-critique of philosophical concepts — their historical and psychological origins, and the danger of mistaking conceptual constructions for eternal truths'

memes.label: Conscience
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of conscience as both a constraint internalized by the herd and a rigorous intellectual virtue demanded of the free spirit'

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: "Courage (Muth, Tapferkeit) as a cardinal virtue of the free spirit in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the intellectual and moral courage to think dangerously, to face uncomfortable truths, and to stand alone."

memes.label: Cruelty
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of cruelty as the hidden foundation of higher culture — the spiritualization of the primordial human instinct to inflict and witness suffering'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Culture
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of culture (Cultur) as the product of aristocratic discipline and hierarchy — distinguished from civilization, democracy, and modern leveling'

memes.label: Curiosity
memes.description: "Nietzsche's distinction between noble and plebeian forms of curiosity (Neugier) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — philosophical curiosity vs. the scholar's submissive historical curiosity."

memes.label: Danger
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of danger as a constitutive element of the philosophical life — living dangerously as the condition of greatness, and the modern flight from danger as a symptom of decadence'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Decadence
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s diagnosis of European modernity as a condition of physiological and cultural decline, characterized by the leveling of values, the triumph of the herd, and the loss of organizing instincts'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Deception
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of deception (Täuschung) in "Beyond Good and Evil" — deception as a fundamental condition of consciousness, a tool of command, and the hidden origin of the will to truth itself.'

memes.label: Democracy
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of the democratic movement in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — democracy as a decay-form of political organization that levels humanity and spreads herd values."

memes.label: Depth
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of psychological depth — the layered soul, the distinction between surface and abyss, and the philosopher''s imperative to think deeply rather than remain on the surface of things'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Desire
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s rehabilitation of desire (Begierde) against the ascetic ideal — desires are not impurities to be extinguished but the raw material of all higher culture and the expression of a being''s will to power'

memes.label: Dogma
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of philosophical dogmatism — the will to make one''s truth a truth for everyone, traced from Plato to the democratic prejudices of modern Europe'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Domination
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of domination-relations as the fundamental structure of morality — life itself understood through relations of command and subordination'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s reconception of duty (Pflicht) beyond Kantian obligation — the philosopher''s duties arise from what he is, not from what he is commanded to do'

memes.label: Education
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of education (Bildung/Erziehung) as self-formation and cultivation — distinguished from modern schooling, which he saw as leveling and domestication'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Essence
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s naturalistic reconception of essence (Wesen) — not a fixed metaphysical substratum but the expression of a being''s ruling drives, its rank, and its mode of valuing'

memes.label: Eternal Recurrence
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s thought experiment: the ultimate test of life-affirmation'

memes.label: Ethical Life
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s implicit critique of the Hegelian concept of Sittlichkeit — ethical life as the product of the tyranny of arbitrary laws, not of nature or laisser-aller'

memes.label: Europe
memes.description: "Nietzsche's diagnosis of European civilization — its history, its sickness, and its potential for unification under the leadership of a new philosophical aristocracy"

memes.label: Exception
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the exceptional individual who breaks free of herd norms — the philosophy of the "Rule" in struggle with the "Exception"'

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: "Nietzsche's analysis of faith (Glaube) as the psychological foundation of metaphysics, morality, and religion — and the demand that philosophy renounce the comfort of belief"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: "Nietzsche's analysis of fear (Furcht) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — fear of the neighbor as the hidden foundation of morality, and the overcoming of fear as the mark of the noble type."

memes.label: Feeling
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of moral feeling (Gefühl) — the primacy of affect in philosophical thought, the plurality of feelings within willing, and the role of feeling in valuation and self-overcoming'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Force
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of force (Kraft) as the accumulated power that bursts moral frameworks, and the principle of least force underlying the will to truth'

memes.label: Form
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of form (Form) as the primordial shaping activity of will to power — the world as a primitive form of the world of affects, and aristocratic society as the condition for giving form to the human type'

memes.label: Free Spirit
memes.description: "Nietzsche's ideal philosophical type — the thinker liberated from traditional moral and metaphysical prejudices who embraces perspectivism and the dangerous life of independent thought"

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of freedom (Freiheit) — freedom as self-mastery and the affect of commanding, the critique of metaphysical "free will," and genuine freedom as the product of long discipline'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: French Revolution
memes.description: 'The French Revolution (1789–1799) as a historical watershed in European morality, discussed by Nietzsche in Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of friendship (Freundschaft) as a philosophical bond of noble souls — distinct from neighbor-love, grounded in shared solitude, and essential to the free spirit''s life'

memes.label: Future
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the future (Zukunft) as the horizon of philosophical legislation — the task of shaping the future human, the future of Europe, and the future of music'

memes.label: Genealogy
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s historical method of tracing moral concepts to their pre-moral origins, revealing how values emerge from power relations rather than from transcendental truth'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Genius
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s typology of genius — the genius as one who either begets or gives birth, distinguished from the scholar by the capacity to create values rather than merely administer knowledge'

memes.label: God
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of the idea of God in Beyond Good and Evil — its psychological origins, its function in the ascetic ideal, and the philosophical consequences of the death of God'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Good European
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s vision of a post-national European identity — the "good European" who transcends national particularism and embodies the continent''s emerging supra-national culture'

memes.label: Habit
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of habit (Gewohnheit) as the psychological mechanism that sustains morality and the will — the habitual feeling that accompanies volition, and the antipodal ears that resist alien truths'

memes.label: Happiness
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of happiness (Glück) in "Beyond Good and Evil" — a restless, waiting condition tied to fortune, timing, and the rare convergence of genius with opportunity.'

memes.label: Health (Great Health)
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of health — particularly 'great health' (grosse Gesundheit) — as a philosophical condition beyond mere physical wellness, encompassing spiritual resilience, nuance of taste, and the capacity to overcome sickness."

memes.label: Heart
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the heart as the seat of the deepest knowledge and suffering — the Genius of the Heart as the Dionysian figure who knows the underworld of every soul'

memes.label: Herd Morality
memes.description: "Nietzsche's diagnosis of modern European morality as herd-animal morality (Heerdenthier-Moral) — the instinct of the gregarious animal that values conformity, safety, and the elimination of fear above all"

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: History
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s philosophy of history — the "historical sense" as both achievement and symptom of modernity, the use and danger of history for life, and genealogy as alternative to historicism'

memes.label: Honesty
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of intellectual honesty (Redlichkeit) as the 'youngest virtue' — the conscience behind the will to truth, and its potential to drive the self-overcoming of morality itself"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Honor
memes.description: "Nietzsche's conception of honor (Ehre) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the philosopher's honor as a counter-value to moral honors distributed by the herd."

memes.label: Immanuel Kant
memes.description: 'German philosopher whose categorical imperative and critique of pure reason Nietzsche challenges throughout Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Instinct
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s naturalistic conception of instinct as the hidden director of conscious thought, including the herd instinct that underlies modern morality'

memes.label: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
memes.description: 'German poet and thinker whom Nietzsche repeatedly invokes in Beyond Good and Evil as an exemplar of noble spirit, life-affirmation, and Dionysian celebration of the affects'

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of justice as a noble virtue — distinct from both revenge and the demand for equality; justice as active fairness'

memes.label: Knowledge
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of knowledge in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the will to knowledge as a form of the will to power, the rejection of 'knowledge for its own sake,' and the figure of the lover of knowledge."

memes.label: Life
memes.description: "Nietzsche's philosophy of life as ultimate value — life as will to power, the perspectival character of all living, and the imperative to 'live according to life' as the reversal of all anti-natural moralities"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Love
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s philosophy of love as possession, self-overcoming, and the highest form of egoism — love beyond pity and Christian agape'

memes.label: Malice
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s moral psychology of malice — not as simple evil but as a productive sharpness of spirit, an intellectual solvent necessary for honest philosophical work'

memes.label: Mask
memes.description: "Nietzsche's philosophy of the mask — the necessity of surface, dissimulation, and protective concealment for the free spirit, and the recognition that depth requires masks."

memes.label: Master-Morality
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the noble, self-affirming morality of masters'

memes.label: Moral Phenomena
memes.description: "Nietzsche's thesis that there are no moral phenomena, only moral interpretations of phenomena — a cornerstone of his perspectivism in 'Beyond Good and Evil'"

memes.label: Morality
memes.description: "Nietzsche's meta-concept for the system of value judgments that structures human life — analyzed historically and psychologically as emerging from relations of power rather than from eternal truth"

memes.label: Music
memes.description: "Nietzsche's philosophy of music in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — music as a cultural diagnostic, the critique of German music, and the dream of a southern, supra-European musical future."

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of nature in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the critique of 'living according to nature,' the natural history of morality, and nature as a normative standard reconsidered."

memes.label: Need
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of need (Noth) as the great socializing force — shared distress enables communication, while the philosopher learns gratitude toward need as a formative condition'

memes.label: Neighbor
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of neighbor-love as a symptom of herd morality — the fear of the neighbor as the origin of moral valuation and the domestication of human relations'

memes.label: Nihilism
memes.description: 'The condition of radical meaninglessness and value-collapse that Nietzsche diagnosed as the consequence of the death of God and the crisis of European morality'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Nobility
memes.description: "Nietzsche's analysis of nobility as a type of human being characterized by self-affirmation, pathos of distance, and the instinct for rank order — the foundation of master-morality"

memes.label: Order of Rank
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s principle that values, types of human beings, and cultures are arranged in a hierarchical order — the denial of which is the cardinal symptom of modern decadence'

memes.label: "Erstes Hauptstück: First Part"
memes.description: "On the Prejudices of Philosophers"

memes.label: "Zweites Hauptstück: Second Part"
memes.description: "The Free Spirit"

memes.label: "Drittes Hauptstück: Third Part"
memes.description: "The Religious Disposition"

memes.label: "Viertes Hauptstück: Fourth Part"
memes.description: "Epigrams and Interludes"

memes.label: "Fünftes Hauptstück: Fifth Part"
memes.description: "Toward a Natural History of Morals"

memes.label: "Sechstes Hauptstück: Sixth Part"
memes.description: "We Scholars"

memes.label: "Siebentes Hauptstück: Seventh Part"
memes.description: "Our Virtues"

memes.label: "Achtes Hauptstück: Eighth Part"
memes.description: "Peoples and Fatherlands"

memes.label: "Neuntes Hauptstück: Ninth Part"
memes.description: "What is Noble?"

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s rehabilitation of the passions against Stoic and Christian suppression — passion as the raw material of greatness, to be disciplined rather than extirpated'

memes.label: Perspectivism
memes.description: "Nietzsche's epistemological doctrine that all knowledge is perspectival — conditioned by the interests, affects, and historical situation of the knower"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Philosopher
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of the philosopher as a type — from dogmatic system-builder to the free-spirited philosopher of the future who creates values rather than seeking eternal truths"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Philosophy
memes.description: "Nietzsche's meta-philosophical reflections in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the prejudices of philosophers, dogmatism as childishness, and the task of philosophy after the death of metaphysical truth"

memes.label: Physiology
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s physiological approach to philosophy — analyzing moral and epistemological positions as expressions of bodily conditions, drives, and racial-cultural formations'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Pity
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of pity (Mitleid) as a core value of slave morality that masks a will to power over the strong and diminishes human greatness"

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Plato
memes.description: 'Ancient Greek philosopher whose idealism and doctrine of Forms Nietzsche identifies as the original dogmatic error of Western philosophy in Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Play
memes.description: "Nietzsche's metaphor of play in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — from the 'bad game' of scholars to the cosmic play of forces shaping the human future"

memes.label: Prejudice
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of philosophical prejudices — the unconscious moral commitments that underlie Western philosophy's claims to objectivity and truth"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of pride (Stolz) as the noble soul's self-affirmation — the belief in oneself and pride in oneself that constitutes the foundation of master morality, opposed to the 'selflessness' prized by slave morality"

memes.label: Psychologist
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s conception of the psychologist in "Beyond Good and Evil" — the psychologist as the premier investigator of the human soul, heir to the philosophical tradition, and the path to the fundamental problems.'

memes.label: Race
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of race (Rasse) as biological-cultural inheritance — the transmission of instincts, values, and physiological dispositions across generations, distinguished from 20th-century racial ideology"

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: "Nietzsche's psychological analysis of the religious disposition — its origins, its role in the economy of human drives, and its function in the history of morality"

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Responsibility
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the philosopher''s responsibility — the "more" that distinguishes the genuine philosopher from the mere scholar'

memes.label: Ressentiment
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the reactive emotion through which the weak moralize their weakness, inventing "evil" to condemn the strong and "good" to sanctify their own impotence'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Revenge
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s analysis of revenge as a moral-psychological motive — distinct from ressentiment as active response rather than passive rumination'

memes.label: Richard Wagner
memes.description: 'German composer whose music and cultural influence Nietzsche both admired and fiercely criticized in Beyond Good and Evil'

memes.label: Right
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of right (Recht) as a privilege of rank — rights are not universal entitlements but expressions of power, earned by those strong enough to claim them and bear their weight'

memes.label: Rise of Christianity
memes.description: 'The historical rise of Christianity as a moral revolution analyzed by Nietzsche in Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Rule
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the Rule as the normative order that philosophy must overcome — the philosophy of the Rule in struggle with the Exception'

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: "Nietzsche's analysis of sacrifice in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — from the psychologist's intellectual sacrifice to Christianity as a system of sacrificial self-abasement"

memes.label: Scholar
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of the scholar type — the scientific specialist as distinct from and inferior to the genuine philosopher, with analysis of the scholar''s psychological characteristics and social function'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Science
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of modern science — its hidden metaphysical presuppositions, its democratic instinct, and its inadequacy as a substitute for genuine philosophy"

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals
Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Self
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the self (Selbst) as embodied, plural, and self-overcoming — neither a unitary soul nor a transcendental ego'

memes.label: Self-Overcoming
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of self-overcoming (Selbstüberwindung) as the continual transcendence of the self — the elevation of the human type through discipline, hardness, and the will to power turned inward"

memes.label: Sense
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s multi-layered concept of "Sinn" — sensory perception, meaning, and the historical sense as tools of philosophical and moral understanding in Beyond Good and Evil'

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: "Nietzsche's analysis of shame (Scham) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — shame as a mark of psychological fineness, its role in the noble soul, and its loss in modern woman."

memes.label: Sign
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the sign (Zeichen) as a mode of social communication grounded in shared experience — the semiotic basis of herd morality and the mechanism by which commonness dominates over rarity'

memes.label: Skepticism
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s ambivalent analysis of skepticism as both a symptom of modern physiological decline and, in its harder form, a prerequisite for the philosopher of the future'

memes.label: Slave-Morality
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of the reactive, ressentiment-driven morality of the oppressed'

memes.label: Society
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of modern society (Gesellschaft) — the tension between individual excellence and social leveling, aristocratic society as the condition for human elevation, and the problem of democracy'

memes.label: Socrates
memes.description: 'Athenian philosopher whose equation of reason with virtue Nietzsche identifies as the pivotal origin of the moral epoch and the suppression of instinct in Western thought'

memes.label: Solitude
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of solitude (Einsamkeit) as the necessary condition of the philosopher — not mere loneliness but the chosen distance from the herd that makes independent thought possible."

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: "Nietzsche's naturalistic reconception of the soul — not as an indestructible metaphysical substance but as a historically bounded, hierarchically structured social multiplicity of drives"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: "Nietzsche's multifaceted concept of spirit (Geist) — the free spirit as philosophical ideal, the historical formation of spirit through discipline and suffering, and the critique of 'German spirit'"

memes.label: Star
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s cosmological metaphor for the loss of transcendent perspective and the plural determination of moral orbits'

memes.label: Struggle
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of struggle (Kampf) as essential to growth — the productive tension between opposing forces that strengthens the noble type and drives philosophical development'

memes.label: Stupidity
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of philosophical stupidity (Dummheit) — not mere ignorance but active resistance to truth, intellectual laziness that produces metaphysical illusions and democratic leveling'

memes.label: Style
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of style (Stil) in "Beyond Good and Evil" — style as the expression of character, the discipline of form, and the mark of cultural health or decline.'

memes.label: Sublimation
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s psychological concept of sublimation — the refinement and spiritualization of raw drives into higher cultural forms'

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Suffering
memes.description: "Nietzsche's valuation of suffering (Leiden) as a discipline and condition for human greatness — suffering as that which deepens, separates, and ennobles, against the modern project of its abolition"

memes.label: Taste
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of taste (Geschmack) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — taste as a faculty of discrimination, a marker of nobility, and a diagnostic of cultural health in Nietzsche's critique of German culture."

memes.label: The Enlightenment
memes.description: 'The European Enlightenment (18th century) as a philosophical movement critically examined by Nietzsche in Beyond Good and Evil'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: The Good
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of "the good-in-itself" (das Gute an sich) — the anti-Platonic rejection of transcendental goodness in favor of perspectival, type-relative value'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: The Holy
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s psychological analysis of the holy, the saint, and holiness — the holy as a riddle of self-conquest and the psychology of ascetic ideals'

memes.label: The Napoleonic Era
memes.description: 'The historical watershed marked by Napoleon Bonaparte that Nietzsche treats as a crucial transition in European political psychology from aristocracy to democracy'

memes.label: The Reformation
memes.description: 'The 16th-century religious movement initiated by Martin Luther that Nietzsche analyzes as a pivotal transformation in European Christianity and the genealogy of modern conscience'

memes.label: Thing-in-Itself
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of the Kantian "thing-in-itself" (Ding an sich) as a contradiction in terms — the noumenon is not a hidden reality but a seduction of language that philosophers must overcome'

memes.label: Thought
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critique of thought (Gedanke, Denken) in "Beyond Good and Evil" — thought as an involuntary process that comes when "it" wills, undermining the Cartesian identification of thinking with the self.'

memes.label: Time
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of historical time in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — modernity as a 'dissolution age,' the 'physicians of the age,' and the philosopher's relationship to his own era"

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: "Nietzsche's critique of the unconditional will to truth as a moral prejudice masking a deeper drive for power and certainty"

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Truthfulness
memes.description: "Nietzsche's concept of truthfulness (Wahrhaftigkeit) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the celebrated yet unexamined philosophical virtue, its relation to the will to truth, and the question of whether anyone has ever been truthful enough about truthfulness itself."

memes.label: Tyrant
memes.description: "Nietzsche's use of 'tyrant' (Tyrann) in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — the tyrant as philosophical actor and the tyranny of arbitrary laws in the genesis of morality."

memes.label: Venture
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s concept of philosophical risk-taking — the willingness to hazard oneself in the pursuit of truth and the reconstruction of human values'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers
Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: Vice
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s rehabilitation of vice (Laster) — not the opposite of virtue but its degeneration under specific conditions, and the mark of a higher, more complex moral psychology beyond simple dualism'

memes.label: Virtue
memes.description: "Nietzsche's revaluation of virtue (Tugend) — the virtues of the free spirit as distinct from traditional moral virtues, with intellectual honesty (Redlichkeit) as the 'youngest virtue' and the one virtue that remains"

relations.label: Part 5: Toward a Natural History of Morals

memes.label: "VORREDE: Preface"
memes.description: "Preface to Beyond Good and Evil"

memes.label: Will to Power
memes.description: 'Central Nietzschean concept: the fundamental drive of all living beings'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Will to Truth
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s critical concept: the philosophical drive to truth is not a pure desire for knowledge but a disguised moral valuation — and may ultimately be a form of the will to power'

relations.label: Part 1: On the Prejudices of Philosophers

memes.label: Woman (das Weib)
memes.description: "Nietzsche's analysis of women and femininity in 'Beyond Good and Evil' — a complex and controversial treatment that intertwines psychological observation, cultural critique, and the philosopher's perspectivism."

memes.label: Word
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s philosophy of words and language — the power, limitations, and deceptive nature of words in philosophical expression and moral valuation'

memes.label: Work
memes.description: 'Nietzsche''s distinction between philosophical workers and genuine philosophers — the labor of scholarship as necessary preparation but not identical with philosophical creation'
